title: nepeta nepetella var. humilis
intro: Nepeta nepetella var. humilis is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its compact, fragrant foliage and small blue flowers. This plant, commonly referred to as Dwarf Catmint, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Nepeta nepetella var. humilis include reducing stress and anxiety, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as colds and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically as a salve or cream for skin irritations and wounds.
